macro_summary: AI infrastructure demand remains the dominant equity theme heading into a packed July catalyst window, with SKHY's historic Nasdaq debut tomorrow set to reprice global memory valuations. Geopolitical risk is re-escalating sharply as the US-Iran ceasefire collapsed July 8, pushing Brent higher and adding an inflationary tail risk ahead of the June CPI print on July 14. The FOMC meets July 29, the same week as MSFT and AAPL earnings, compressing multiple macro and micro catalysts into a 3-week window. Sector rotation and rate-sensitivity headwinds remain the primary risk to tech positioning.
screening_note: 6 total overnight screening runs analyzed; minimum threshold to qualify was 2 appearances. All 5 ideas are included. SKHY and NVDA achieved perfect 6/6 consensus; AAPL and MSFT appeared in 5/6 runs; XLE cleared the minimum at 2/6.
key_events: Iran oil sanctions waiver hard expiry: July 17, 2026
MSFT Q4 FY2026 earnings: July 28, 2026 after close
SKHY new share issuance on Korean exchange: July 29, 2026
NVDA Q2 FY2027 earnings: August 26, 2026 after close

ideas.name: SK Hynix Inc. American Depositary Shares
ideas.thesis: Appeared in all 6 overnight screens with 5 high-conviction votes — the strongest consensus of any idea this cycle. Tomorrow's Nasdaq debut is the largest ADR listing in history (~$29.4B), removing the structural 'Korea Discount' that has kept the world's dominant HBM supplier (56-58% market share, HBM supply sold out through 2027) inaccessible to most US institutions. HSBC applied a 20% P/B premium re-rating on listing accessibility alone, and Mirae estimates $1.5B+ in forced ETF inflows upon Philadelphia Semiconductor Index inclusion targeted for late July.
ideas.catalyst: Nasdaq trading commences July 10, 2026 (tomorrow); potential fast-track PHLX Semiconductor Index inclusion by late July 2026; new Korean share issuance dilution event July 29, 2026
ideas.edge: Q1 2026 revenue +198% YoY with >70% operating margin; trades at a ~35% historical discount to Micron (Korea accessibility friction now being removed); IPO price ~$158/ADS implies 20.55x P/E vs. NVDA at 30-40x; HSBC 20% P/B re-rating premium; $1.5B+ estimated forced ETF inflows on index inclusion
ideas.bull_case: US institutional access unlocks a multi-year re-rating toward US semi peer multiples; index inclusion inflows create a near-term technical tailwind on top of fundamentally elite HBM economics with no options available to hedge shorts on day one.
ideas.bear_case: IPO-day volatility can be severe on large debut listings; Korean shares have already surged ~710% in 12 months, limiting further re-rating runway; the July 29 new share issuance on the Korean exchange is a known dilution overhang. No options available at day-one open, limiting hedging flexibility.

ideas.name: NVIDIA Corporation
ideas.thesis: Appeared in all 6 overnight screens, making it a unanimous consensus idea. NVDA's forward P/E has compressed to ~22x — a 7-year low and a fraction of its 5-year average of 72x — despite Q1 FY2027 revenue of $81.6B (+85% YoY) and a confirmed August 26 earnings date with VeraRubin production ramp and HBM4 supply qualification as concrete catalysts. Analyst consensus implies ~53% upside to the $301 avg price target from current levels near $197, which is ~17% below its 52-week high.
ideas.catalyst: Q2 FY2027 earnings confirmed August 26, 2026 after close; TSMC (July 16) and Intel (July 23) prints as near-term AI capex read-throughs; CPI July 14 and FOMC July 29 as macro gating events
ideas.edge: Forward P/E of ~22x vs. 5-year average of 72x and vs. AAPL at 37x despite 5x faster growth; EPS consensus Q2 FY2027 at $2.08; analyst avg price target implies ~53% upside; VeraRubin ramp and HBM supply sold out through most of 2027 support demand visibility
ideas.bull_case: Historically cheap multiple with elite growth and a hard earnings date in 7 weeks; TSMC/Intel prints could act as positive AI capex catalysts before the NVDA print, pulling the stock higher in advance.
ideas.bear_case: Ongoing Iran-driven macro volatility and tech sector rotation could suppress the stock through July; the 7-week wait to the Aug 26 catalyst is a long hold in a choppy tape, and any AI capex softness in partner earnings would be a headwind.

ideas.name: Apple Inc.
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 5 of 6 overnight screens with 4 high-conviction votes. AAPL has a hard July 30 earnings date with strong forward momentum: Q2 FY2026 revenue grew 17% YoY to $111.2B with a 22% iPhone sales jump, and management guided 14-17% revenue growth for Q3. A rumored 5-iPhone lineup including a ~$2,500 foldable device and Tim Cook's final earnings call provide secondary catalysts. All three runs recommended a defined-risk call spread rather than outright stock given the stretched 37x earnings multiple.
ideas.catalyst: Q3 FY2026 earnings confirmed July 30, 2026; foldable iPhone product lineup announcement expected this fall; Tim Cook final earnings call narrative
ideas.edge: Q2 revenue +17% YoY to $111.2B; iPhone sales +22%; guided 14-17% Q3 revenue growth above prior consensus; stock added ~4.8% on expanded iPhone lineup report; AAPL is only ~4% behind NVDA in market cap, sustaining large-cap momentum flows
ideas.bull_case: Strong beat-and-raise cadence heading into the print, foldable iPhone buzz adding a product-cycle premium, and a clean July 30 binary catalyst make this a high-probability pre-earnings options trade.
ideas.bear_case: 37x forward earnings is a rich entry; any guide-down or foldable launch delay would hit a crowded long hard; macro rate shock from CPI/FOMC combo in the week before earnings could compress multiples ahead of the print.

ideas.name: Microsoft Corporation
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 5 of 6 overnight screens. MSFT is trading at its cheapest forward multiple since 2023 (~22x NTM earnings vs. analyst consensus targets implying 34-56% upside) while carrying three converging catalysts: Q4 FY2026 earnings July 28, Azure guidance of 39-40% CC growth, and the margin inflection signal from 4,800 AI-driven job cuts announced July 6. All three screening runs recommended a defined-risk call spread into the July 28 print, contingent on the July 14 CPI not delivering a hawkish shock.
ideas.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings confirmed July 28, 2026 after close; June CPI July 14 as macro gating event; ex-dividend August 20, 2026
ideas.edge: Analyst consensus price target ~$589-$592 implying ~56% upside; trading at 21-22x NTM earnings — cheapest since 2023; Q3 EPS beat consensus by 5.5%; EPS forecast to grow 44-46% over next 3 years; estimated fair value gap of ~25% vs. current ~$424 price
ideas.bull_case: Compressed multiple, consistent beat history, accelerating Azure AI growth, and a margin expansion thesis from workforce restructuring combine to create a high-probability upside setup into the July 28 print.
ideas.bear_case: A hawkish CPI surprise July 14 or FOMC surprise July 29 could reprice rate-sensitive large-cap tech before earnings arrive; class action litigation overhang is an unquantified tail risk; AI cost restructuring could signal demand softness if misread by the market.

ideas.name: Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 2 of 6 runs — minimum threshold, but both were high-conviction votes tied to a concrete geopolitical timeline. The US-Iran ceasefire collapsed July 8, US strikes are ongoing, and the Iranian oil sanctions waiver hard-expires July 17 — removing Iranian crude from global supply at a structurally tight moment. XLE was the sole green sector (+2.4%) in Wednesday's broad selloff, WTI surged ~7% this week, and XLE is already +24% YTD. Both runs flagged this as a dual directional and portfolio hedge idea into the July 14 CPI print.
ideas.catalyst: Iranian oil sanctions waiver hard expiry July 17, 2026; June CPI release July 14, 2026; ongoing US-Iran military conflict with Hormuz transit risk re-escalating
ideas.edge: XLE sole green sector (+2.4%) on Wednesday broad selloff; WTI +7% this week to ~$75/barrel; Brent at $78.85 and rising for 3 consecutive sessions; IMF forecasts oil prices +32% in 2026; top holdings Exxon (+22%) and Chevron (+17%) provide fundamental support
ideas.bull_case: Hard supply-shock catalyst (July 17 sanctions expiry) layered on active military conflict in a region controlling ~20% of global oil transit creates a durable bid; energy-driven CPI upside on July 14 would further validate the thesis and push XLE higher.
ideas.bear_case: Oil geopolitics can de-escalate rapidly on diplomatic breakthroughs, reversing the spike; at +24% YTD, much of the move may be priced in; a broader risk-off episode could hit energy equities even if crude holds, as happened in prior cycles.
